title: What is a database migration?
prompt_md: What is a database **migration**, and why would you version your schema with migration scripts instead of editing tables by hand?
options: A migration is a versioned, ordered script describing a schema change; versioning makes the schema reproducible across environments, reviewable in git, and safely reversible
A migration moves your database from one cloud provider to another and has nothing to do with table structure
A migration is a cache of frequently-run queries that speeds up reads
A migration is a backup of all your data taken before deploying, used only for disaster recovery
explanation_md: A migration is a small, ordered, version-controlled script (e.g. "add column `phone` to `users`") run by tools like Alembic. Versioning your schema means every environment reaches the same structure by replaying the same scripts, changes are reviewable in a PR, and they can be rolled back — far safer than ad-hoc manual `ALTER TABLE` on a live database. It is like git history for your schema. The other options describe backups, infra moves, and caching — none of which are migrations.
